name: PLIANT THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: PLIANT THERAPEUTICS is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering and developing integrin-based therapeutics. Its lead product candidate, PLN-101095, is an oral, small molecule dual inhibitor of αvβ8 and αvβ1 integrins for the treatment of solid tumors resistant to immune checkpoint inhibitors. The company also has a proprietary drug discovery platform with over 15,000 integrin binding molecules for targeted drug delivery.
sellsTo: patients with solid tumors resistant to immune checkpoint inhibitors
potential patients for integrin-targeted drug delivery in multiple disease areas

exposures.sentence: Our lead program is focused on the development of treatments for solid tumors by inhibiting integrin-mediated activation of transforming growth factor- (TGF- ).
Our wholly owned lead product candidate, PLN-101095, is an oral, small molecule, dual selective inhibitor of v 8 and v 1 integrins in development for the treatment of solid tumors.

We also continue to explore the full potential of our drug discovery platform consisting of a proprietary library of over 15,000 integrin binding molecules, focusing on integrin-based target-mediated drug delivery.
Our approach to drug discovery and development of integrin-based therapeutics is unproven and may not result in a marketable product.

catalysts.event: Data from Phase 1 trial of PLN-101095 to be presented at AACR Annual Meeting 2026
catalysts.sentence: Data from the Phase 1 trial of PLN-101095 will be the subject of a poster presentation and an oral presentation as part of the Clinical Trials Minisymposium at the upcoming American Association of Cancer Research (AACR) conference to be held April 17-22, 2026, in San Diego, California.

catalysts.event: First patient enrollment in Phase 1b indication expansion trial of PLN-101095
catalysts.sentence: Study start activities for this trial are underway with first patient enrollment anticipated in second quarter.

catalysts.event: Interim data from Phase 1b indication expansion trial of PLN-101095
